msgs.text:
I treated the prior file as a list of external proposals. The prompt fixes the four-generator tame format, with sigma,tau distinguished, the tame relation tau^sigma = tau^2 implicit, later generators in the pro-2 core, and constants reduced modulo M=85667662080.The only live candidate from the prior investigations is still the Turn 9–19 dynamic tame-inertia word; the earlier candidates were refuted by explicit finite-tame or index-two tests in the prior record.

The live candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with π=π2 and η=2π on tame inertia,r=xσ−πxσπx−1(xτ−η)π ((yτ)π)σy−1,where π≡40491355905,−π≡45176306175,−η=−2π ≡50932630783(modM).My new check concerns Turn 19’s “infinite Frobenius ambiguity” claim. The claim was directionally right but incomplete: Turn 19 exhibited non-boundary classes in the raw graded C2 -module, but it did not check that those classes preserve the Demuškin initial relator one layer further. That extra condition is essential for a Frobenius-action deformation to be meaningful.Specialize to the unramified quadratic quotient and kill τ. The candidate becomesr=xσ−1 xσ[σ,y].

For the index-two pro-2 kernel U, use the prior notationa0=x0,a1=x1,b=y0x1,c=S=σ2.On the associated restricted Lie algebra L=gr(U), the quotient Frobenius α swaps a0,a1 and fixes b,c. The Demuškin initial relation isq=a0[2]+a1[2]+[b,c].Putz=[a0,a1].

Turn 19 considered the classesdn (b)=z[2n],dn(a0)=dn(a1)=dn (c)=0.As written, these do not preserve q one layer further, becausedn (q)=[z[2n],c],which is generally nonzero. So the pure classes in Turn 19 are not yet genuine relation-preserving Frobenius deformations.The important point is that there is a canonical correction. Definetn=(adz)2n−1(c)with the convention t0=c, and setδn (b)=z[2n],δn(a0)=[a1,tn],δn(a1)=[a0,tn],δn (c)=0.

Then δn is α-equivariant: α swaps the two displayed a-terms and fixes b,c,z,tn. More importantly, it preserves the initial Demuškin relator to the next graded layer:δn(q)=[a0,[a1,tn]]+[a1,[a0,tn]]+[z[2n],c]=[z,tn]+[z[2n],c]=(adz)2n (c)+[z[2n],c]=0.The second equality is Jacobi in characteristic 2, and the third is the restricted-Lie identity (adz)2n=ad(z[2n]).For n=1, this says the degree-four class from Turn 18 should not be represented purely by d(b)=z[2]. A relation-preserving representative isδ1 (b)=z[2],δ1(a0)=[a1,[z,c]],δ1(a1)=[a0,[z,c]],δ1 (c)=0.This exactly explains the missing correction term at the first higher layer.The non-boundary part of Turn 19’s argument survives. Mapa0↦E12,a1↦E21,b,c↦0 inside gl2(F2), with α acting by transpose. Then q↦0, whilez=[a0,a1]↦I2,soz[2n]↦I2 for every n≥0. But (α+1)gl2(F2) consists of matrices of the form A+At, hence has zero diagonal. Therefore I2 is not an (α+1)-boundary. Thus each corrected δn gives a nonzero marked C2 -cohomology class at the corresponding graded level.I also did a finite row-reduction check in degrees 4 and 5. Before imposing relator preservation, Turn 18’s degree-four calculation gives six marked module-cohomology classes. After imposing the next-layer condition δ(q)=0 and quotienting by relation-preserving marked boundaries, the degree-four ambiguity has dimension 4, not 6. The class above, represented byδ1 (b)=z[2],δ1(a0)=[a1,[z,c]],δ1(a1)=[a0,[z,c]],is one of the four surviving classes.So Turn 19’s conclusion should be sharpened, not discarded. The infinite tower is not an arbitrary tower of pure d(b)=z[2n]perturbations. It is a structured tower of symplectic/relation-preserving derivation classes δn. This makes the remaining formation gap more concrete: a final proof must match these corrected classes, or prove a rigidity theorem that determines their values from the displayed global relation.

ESTABLISHED: The live Turn 9–19 candidate remains unrefuted. Turn 19’s raw classes dn (b)=z[2n]were incomplete because they did not preserve the Demuškin relator one layer further. I found the canonical corrected relation-preserving representativesδn (b)=z[2n],δn(a0)=[a1,(adz)2n−1c],δn(a1)=[a0,(adz)2n−1c],δn (c)=0,and proved δn (q)=0. These classes remain non-boundary.

GAP: The candidate is still not a proved presentation of GQ2. The remaining dyadic formation problem now has a sharper form: the full Frobenius action must be matched against this corrected infinite tower of relation-preserving classes, not just against the Frattini module, cup product, Bockstein class, or the first D4 descent bit.

NEXT: Compute the actual values selected by the candidate on the corrected classes δn, beginning with the four-dimensional degree-four space, and identify the corresponding arithmetic Kummer/embedding-problem invariants over the unramified quadratic extension.[BREAKTHROUGH]
